careers.title: Education
careers.tagline: Shape minds, inspire learners, and build the future of South Africa.
careers.description: Education careers help learners grow from school through tertiary study and beyond. Paths include classroom teaching, curriculum design, educational psychology, and EdTech roles.
careers.longDescription: Teaching is one of the most impactful careers in South Africa. Educators work in primary schools, high schools, universities, and training centres. The country needs passionate, qualified teachers—especially in STEM subjects and rural areas where quality education remains scarce. Whether you dream of inspiring a classroom or designing national curricula, education offers stable employment, community impact, and deep personal fulfillment.

careers.title: Natural Sciences
careers.tagline: Research, discover, and drive innovation in labs and the field.
careers.description: Science careers span biology, chemistry, physics, environmental science, and data-driven fields like actuarial science. Work in labs, hospitals, and R&D teams solving real-world problems.
careers.longDescription: South Africa has a growing science sector with opportunities in mining research, biotechnology, environmental monitoring, and pharmaceutical development. Scientists are needed in government agencies like the CSIR (Council for Scientific and Industrial Research) and SANSA, as well as private companies and academia. The country's biodiversity and unique geological features make it a hub for environmental and geological research.

careers.title: Psychology
careers.tagline: Understand behaviour, support wellbeing, and apply evidence-based practice.
careers.description: Psychology careers range from counselling and clinical work to organisational and educational psychology. Professionals assess needs, deliver interventions, and collaborate across health, education, and workplace settings.
careers.longDescription: Mental health awareness is growing rapidly in South Africa, creating demand for qualified psychologists, counsellors, and social workers. Clinical psychologists work in hospitals and private practice; organisational psychologists partner with companies on talent management and employee wellness. Community psychologists are vital in townships and rural areas where mental health services are severely under-resourced. HPCSA registration is required for professional practice.

careers.title: Commerce & Finance
careers.description: Commerce connects markets, customers, and capital. Specialise in accounting, finance, marketing, or entrepreneurship—often moving between analysis, strategy, and client-facing roles.
careers.longDescription: South Africa's economy is the most developed on the continent, with Johannesburg serving as Africa's financial capital. Commerce graduates are in demand in banking, insurance, consulting, and the fast-growing fintech sector. CA(SA) (Chartered Accountant South Africa) is one of the most respected professional qualifications, opening doors globally. The Big Four accounting firms (Deloitte, PwC, EY, KPMG) all recruit heavily from SA universities.

careers.title: Sports Science
careers.tagline: Performance coaching, analytics, sports medicine, and wellness.
careers.description: Sports careers combine passion with science: coaching, strength and conditioning, sports analytics, and sports business management. Roles exist in schools, clubs, federations, and the wellness industry.
careers.longDescription: South Africa's sports culture—rugby, football, cricket, and athletics—creates a vibrant career ecosystem. Sports scientists work with national teams, Olympic athletes, and provincial academies. The wellness and fitness industry is booming, with gyms, biokineticists, and performance coaches in high demand. Sports management professionals run stadiums, events, and commercial partnerships worth billions.

careers.title: Art & Design
careers.tagline: Visual storytelling, UX design, film, and creative direction.
careers.description: Creative careers blend craft with technology: graphic design, UX/UI, illustration, film, animation, and fashion. Portfolios matter as much as formal qualifications.
careers.longDescription: South Africa has a vibrant creative economy, with the advertising, gaming, and film industries growing rapidly. Cape Town is a global film destination, and Johannesburg's creative quarter drives African design culture. Digital skills are increasingly essential—UX designers, motion graphic artists, and brand strategists who understand tech are especially sought after. The country's diverse cultural heritage makes South African designers uniquely competitive globally.
institutions: UCT Michaelis School of Fine Art

careers.title: Humanities & Law
careers.tagline: Research, policy, journalism, and the pursuit of justice.
careers.description: Humanities graduates excel at research, argument, and communication—valuable in law, journalism, public policy, NGOs, and academia.
careers.longDescription: South Africa's democracy and constitutional framework create strong demand for lawyers, policy analysts, journalists, and social researchers. Legal careers range from practicing advocate to corporate counsel; many top politicians and business leaders hold law degrees. Journalism remains vital in a country where press freedom is highly valued. Social researchers help government and NGOs understand and address inequality, poverty, and social development challenges.

careers.title: Information Technology
careers.tagline: Build software, systems, and digital solutions for Africa and beyond.
careers.description: Software developers, data scientists, cybersecurity analysts, and cloud engineers are in extreme demand across every sector of the South African economy.
careers.longDescription: South Africa's tech sector is booming—Cape Town is dubbed Africa's Silicon Valley and Johannesburg hosts major fintech hubs. IT graduates are among the most employable in the country, with many earning competitive salaries and working remotely for international companies. Key growth areas include cybersecurity, data science, artificial intelligence, and mobile app development for Africa's massive smartphone market. Bootcamp graduates and self-taught developers who build strong portfolios also thrive.

careers.title: Engineering
careers.tagline: Design the infrastructure, machines, and systems powering South Africa.
careers.description: Engineering spans civil, mechanical, electrical, mining, and chemical fields. Engineers solve the country's biggest infrastructure and energy challenges.
careers.longDescription: South Africa's infrastructure needs—roads, water systems, power grids, and mining operations—require thousands of engineers. The Eskom power crisis has made electrical and energy engineers critical. Civil engineers are essential for the government's housing and infrastructure programmes. Mining engineers are needed at some of the world's largest gold, platinum, and coal operations. Engineering is a protected profession regulated by ECSA (Engineering Council of South Africa).

careers.title: Health Sciences
careers.tagline: Medicine, nursing, pharmacy, and allied health professions.
careers.description: Health science careers serve South Africa's vast healthcare needs. Doctors, nurses, pharmacists, physiotherapists, and many allied professionals are urgently needed.
careers.longDescription: South Africa faces a critical shortage of healthcare professionals, especially in public hospitals and rural areas. Medical graduates can expect strong employment but must complete community service years. Nursing is one of the most needed professions—the government trains and employs nurses at scale through the National School of Nursing. Pharmacists are in demand in retail, hospital, and industrial settings. The HIV/AIDS epidemic has created specialised roles in infectious disease management.
